LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUMENT IS HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. A BAD FAITH BREACH CLAIM CAN GIVE RISE TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (SEE, E.G., MAZIK V. GEICO GENERAL INS. CO. (2019) 35 CAL.APP.5TH 455 [AFFIRMING JUDGMENT AWARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST INSURER FOR BAD FAITH BREACH OF INSURANCE CONTRACT].) (SEE WORD MINUTES THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE IN CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

Atty. Elizabeth C. Papay, Esq. of Denton's US LLP, 415.882.0135, appearing for Defendant Allstate Insurance Company;
Atty. John C. McCaslin, Esq., of McCaslin Law PC, 916.200.8455, appearing for Plaintiff Carolyn McCone.

The matter is called and passed. At the second call of the matter, ARGUMENT is held.

DEFENDANT ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Allstate's demurrer to the first amended complaint is overruled as to the first, second, fourth, and fifth causes of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action.

(See Word minutes this date for the Court's complete ruling.)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance in CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Elizabeth C. Papay, Esq. of Denton's US LLP, 415.882.0135, appearing for Defendant Allstate Insurance Company;
Atty. John C. McCaslin, Esq., of McCaslin Law PC, 916.200.8455, appearing for Plaintiff Carolyn McCone.

The matter is called and passed. At the second call of the matter, ARGUMENT is held.

DEFENDANT ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion to strike the request for punitive damages from the first amended complaint is denied. A bad faith breach claim can give rise to punitive damages. (See, e.g., Mazik v. Geico General Ins. Co. (2019) 35 Cal.App.5th 455 [affirming judgment awarding punitive damages against insurer for bad faith breach of insurance contract].)

(See Word minutes this date for the Court's complete ruling.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance in CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
